author: __kasey__
content: <@504202741933932544> re attestations at the fork for 7688, the serialization doesn't change for progressive containers, and so for the most part this should just be a matter of converting/casting values when integrated into a container that needs to merkleize them as progressive. I'm just checking to see if I missed any other nuances there.

author: tbenr
content: yes i did the conversion, not tested yet 🙂

author: pk910
content:
rel 7688:
Is `BeaconState.historical_summaries` still expected to be a List? all other Lists were converted to ProgressiveLists

author: __kasey__
content: Different topic: I think one of the bigger bug risks with 7688 is moving to checking list bounds in STF rather than enforcing in types (which naturally rejects oversized values at unmarshaling time). I'm considering integrating those validations directly into unmarshaling so we still get that protection at the edge.

author: tbenr
content: another thing i want to see is the rebuilding of participation lists list->progressiveList at state upgrade.. and how we are good int the mutable progressive list. I don't remember ATM.

author: etan_status
content: the limits are in the presets file 🙂

author: etan_status
content:
currently they are. but if there is consensus I'm also open to mark them progressive, don't want to block on this, though.

https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7688#history-accumulators
> `historical_roots` and `historical_summaries` are deliberately kept as `List` types. `historical_roots` has been frozen since Capella, and `historical_summaries` is actively used by verifiers proving against its `hash_tree_root`; converting either of them now would add churn or break verifiers without sufficient benefit. A future EIP can address both, potentially consolidating them into a single accumulator alongside related cleanups.

author: etan_status
content: it's just a typecast. same serialization / same limits as before

author: etan_status
content: yeah, the fork transition block will need measurements. right now, that's an open detail I don't have an answer for. maybe one of the devnets could be run with a large validator set (1M+) to mimic mainnet. a simple approach could be to temporarily hold two copies and maintain both in the epochs leading to the fork transition, and then drop that code again when gloas finalizes.

author: tbenr
content: once i complete the first round, i'll fake a transition from mainnet or hoodi and check
